meta_title: Puppy Cost Singapore: Full First-Year Budget | Curious Tails
meta_description: How much does a puppy really cost in Singapore? Purchase paths from $70 adoption to $4,488 licensed all-in, plus the honest first-year budget: $1,200 to $3,500 on top.
h1: How Much Does a Puppy Cost in Singapore? The Full Year

entity_definitions.definition: A puppy in Singapore costs its purchase price plus a first year of running costs: the AVS licence from $15 a year, food $340 to $2,430, routine vet care $300 to $500, grooming around $274 for low-shedding coats, and optional insurance from about $316. A realistic first-year planning figure is $1,200 to $3,500 on top of the purchase.

top_competitor_weaknesses: no bridge from cost table to an actual buying decision
information_gaps_none_cover: month-one shopping list priced (and what a kit removes)
factual_conflicts: 'lifetime ~$51,166 (PetBacker)' vs simpler annual views → use annual structure, cite ranges not lifetime shock numbers

faq.question: How much does a puppy cost per month in Singapore?
faq.answer_direction: $100 to $290 a month covers food, care and grooming for a small breed once purchase is done.

faq.question: What does the AVS licence cost?
faq.answer_direction: From $15/year; one-time $35 for sterilised dogs; we complete it at purchase.

faq.question: Is pet insurance worth it?
faq.answer_direction: From ~$316/year; weigh it against a $2,000+ emergency fund; either strategy works, having neither doesn't.

faq.question: What's the single biggest avoidable cost?
faq.answer_direction: The unlicensed 'bargain': $500-$1,000 saved upfront against $5,000-$10,000 parvo bills and a $7,000 fine precedent.

faq.question: What does the starter kit save me?
faq.answer_direction: About $500 of day-one equipment, plus the mispurchases first-timers make buying blind.

faq.question: Grooming: how much and how often?
faq.answer_direction: Low-shedding coats: every 4 to 8 weeks, ~$274/year average.
